summary: Split the old fighter assembly range so the 0x800881D8-0x800892A0 portion became src/melee/ft/ft_0881.c, leaving the later remainder in asm/melee/ft/ft_0892.s, and added new ft_0881.h/ft_0892.h headers plus widespread include updates. The report claims 13.12% matched for the new 4.6 KB ft_0881 unit, with some small functions/inlines fully converted while many functions still use ASM fallbacks or NOT_IMPLEMENTED non-MUST_MATCH branches.
what_changed: Created src/melee/ft/ft_0881.c with 1354 added lines covering functions from ft_800881D8 through ft_800892A0, including C for helper inlines/functions and embedded ASM blocks for functions not yet fully matched.
Renamed/split asm/melee/ft/ft_0877.s into asm/melee/ft/ft_0892.s and deleted roughly 1203 lines from the assembly file corresponding to the new ft_0881.c range.
Updated obj_files.mk so the build now compiles src/melee/ft/ft_0881.c.o and the remaining asm/melee/ft/ft_0892.s.o instead of only asm/melee/ft/ft_0877.s.o.
Added src/melee/ft/ft_0881.h for declarations in the 0881D8-0892A0 range and src/melee/ft/ft_0892.h for later declarations beginning at ft_800892D4.
Trimmed src/melee/ft/ft_0877.h so it mostly retains earlier ft_0877 declarations and moved later prototypes into the new headers; declarations use address comments such as /* 0881D8 */.
Added ft_0881.h and/or ft_0892.h includes across many fighter common and character source files that depended on functions formerly declared in ft_0877.h.

Adjusted types around FtCollisionData and Fighter fields: FtCollisionData became a named struct forward-declared in ft/forward.h, x0/x4 were typed as int/int*, Fighter x2068_attackID became uint, x206C/x206E were split into shorts, and x2144 became int.
Updated db_2253.h to use GALE01_225374 header guard, include ft/forward.h and it/forward.h, type item/owner parameters as Item_GObj* and Fighter_GObj*, and add address comments to declarations.
Made small signature/type cleanups in related headers and ground code, including Ground_801C5414(int arg0, int arg1).
In ft_0877.c, added a comment naming ft_80087C70 as Player_AdjustSFXIDForSizeModifier and rewrote switch cases from hex literals to decimal literals while preserving behavior.
smart_moves: Separated the partially decompiled range from the still-assembly tail, allowing ft_0881.c to build as C while keeping unmatched ft_0892 functions isolated in asm.
Kept build order explicit in obj_files.mk and inserted new TEXT_O_FILES groups, reducing risk from object ordering changes in a decomp project.
Used new headers to avoid overloading ft_0877.h with declarations from multiple address ranges, then propagated includes to direct users instead of relying on stale transitive declarations.
Used address-commented declarations in headers, which makes unresolved symbol ranges searchable and helps future splits know where prototypes belong.

Forward-declared FtCollisionData in ft/forward.h and converted it from typedef-anonymous style to a named struct, making pointer declarations in new ft_0881.h possible without pulling full type definitions everywhere.

Preserved assembly for not-yet-matching functions under ASM/nofralloc blocks while exposing WIP C or NOT_IMPLEMENTED branches, letting the file compile and progress incrementally without claiming full C matches.
Factored repeated sound-start logic into inlineA0/inlineB0/inlineC0 in the C source where the report shows these helpers or adjacent small functions contributed to the matched percentage.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large assembly object, move only the proven C range into a new source file and leave the remaining address range in a renamed asm file; update object lists and prototypes in the same PR.
Header splits need broad include repair: many fighter/chara files were updated because declarations previously available from ft_0877.h moved to ft_0881.h or ft_0892.h.
Named structs plus forward declarations are useful for cross-module prototypes such as ft_800889F4(Fighter* fp, FtCollisionData* coll), especially when the complete struct is in ft/types.h.

For SFX/audio fighter code, offsets such as fp->x2144 through fp->x215C store lbAudioAx_80023870 handles, and channel/group arguments are derived from player_id, x221F_b4, and constants like 0x1E/0x2A/0x36.

The code pattern around special SFX IDs 0x3D60 and 0x3D61 recurs across ft_800881D8, ft_80088328, ft_80088478, ft_80088510, ft_800885A8, ft_80088640, and ftCo_800886D8; future matches can reuse the switch/addis idiom carefully.
The report evidence is limited to a PR dump with no reviewer comments, so lessons about reviewer intent or final naming approval are weak.
naming_conventions: New fighter split headers use address-based guards: GALE01_0881D8 for ft_0881.h and GALE01_0892D4 for ft_0892.h.
Function declarations in headers use address comments before prototypes, e.g. /* 0881D8 */ void ft_800881D8(...).
The common fighter-prefixed symbol ftCo_800886D8 remains named with ftCo_, while most other functions in this range retain address-based ft_8008xxxx names.
Temporary helper names inlineA0, inlineB0, and inlineC0 were used for local matching helpers rather than invented semantic names.
Field names remain decomp placeholder style such as x2068_attackID, x2225_b6, x221F_b4, x2144, and x2070.x2071_b5, with a small semantic improvement for x2068_attackID.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used ASM function declarations with #if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP) C/NOT_IMPLEMENTED branches and #else nofralloc assembly blocks for exact matching builds.
Kept raw assembly labels and instructions inside ft_0881.c for unmatched functions, followed by #pragma peephole on after blocks.
Converted a subset of simple routines to C, including ft_800889F4, ft_800890BC, ft_800890D0, ft_800892A0, and local inlines, while larger routines remained assembly-backed.
Used explicit casts like (s8) arg2 and (s8) arg3 in inlineA0 to match the clrlwi/lbz-style argument narrowing seen in assembly.
Represented the assembly addis/cmplwi SFX sentinel check as switch (arg1 + 0xFFF80000) with cases 0x3D60U and 0x3D61U in inlineA0.
Preserved the original sdata2 float literal as C: /* literal */ float const ft_804D83E0 = 1;
Moved the matched text out of asm by deleting the 0x800881D8-0x800892A0 symbols from asm/melee/ft/ft_0892.s while preserving the later assembly tail.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompile the ASM-backed functions in src/melee/ft/ft_0881.c, especially ft_80088328 through ft_80088C5C.
Search for subsequent renames or semantic discoveries for ft_800881D8, ft_80088770, ft_800887CC, and ftCo_800886D8.
Check later PRs involving FtCollisionData to see whether the int/int* field typing introduced here was refined.
Look for review or follow-up changes around the broad ft_0881.h/ft_0892.h include propagation to see if include minimization was later improved.
Search for uses of the SFX sentinel constants 0x3D60/0x3D61 and lbAudioAx_80023870 channel computations in other fighter audio matching work.
